vocabulary.example_pinyin: yī ge xīngqī yǒu qī tiān.

vocabulary.translation: I'll be there for two weeks.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Excuse me, what time is it now?

lines.speaker: B
lines.pinyin: Nǐ hǎo! Xiànzài jǐ diǎn le?
lines.english: Hello! What time is it now?

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 现在上午九点。你什么时候来？
lines.pinyin: Xiànzài shàngwǔ jiǔ diǎn. Nǐ shénme shíhou lái?
lines.english: It's 9 AM now. When are you coming?

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 有。我们中午一起吃饭吧！
lines.pinyin: Yǒu. Wǒmen zhōngwǔ yì qǐ chīfàn ba!

lines.speaker: A
lines.pinyin: Tīng shuō, nǐ jīnnián qù Zhōngguó?
lines.english: I heard you're going to China this year?

lines.speaker: B
lines.pinyin: Bù, wǒ míngnián yīyuè yí rì qù.
lines.english: No, I'm going on January 1st next year.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 太好了!我会打电话给你。
lines.pinyin: Tài hǎo le! wǒ huì dǎ diànhuà gěi nǐ.

grammar_points.title: Telling Clock Time: X点Y分
grammar_points.explanation: To tell clock time: [Time of day] + [Number] + 点(diǎn) + [Minutes] + 分(fēn). Minutes are optional if on the hour. 半(bàn) = half past. Examples: 三点(3:00), 三点半(3:30), 三点十分(3:10).

exercises.question: Fill in: 现在中午___点。(It is now 12:00 noon)

mini_exercises.question: Which correctly tells the time '10:20 AM'?

grammar_points.title: 什么时候 (shénme shíhou) — When
grammar_points.explanation: 什么时候(shénme shíhou) asks about when something happens. It is placed before the verb, like all time expressions in Chinese. Formula: Subject + 什么时候 + Verb?

examples.english: When are you going to China?

exercises.question: Ask 'When does she come?' in Chinese
Where does 什么时候 go in the sentence?

mini_exercises.question: Fill in the blank:
mini_exercises.question_english: When are you going to China?

grammar_points.title: Days of the Week: 星期 + Number
grammar_points.explanation: 星期(xīngqī) + 1-6 = Monday to Saturday. 星期天(tiān)/日(rì) = Sunday. 星期几(jǐ)? = What day of the week? Numbers 1-6 go after 星期.

grammar_points.title: Counting Weeks: Number + 个星期
grammar_points.explanation: To count weeks in Chinese, use: Number + 个(gè) + 星期(xīngqī). 个 is the measure word for weeks. Examples: 一个星期(one week), 两个星期(two weeks), 三个星期(three weeks). This pattern works for any number.

examples.pinyin: Yī gè xīngqī yǒu qī tiān.

examples.chinese: 我在中国待了两个星期。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zài Zhōngguó dāi le liǎng gè xīngqī.
examples.english: I stayed in China for two weeks.

exercises.question: How do you say 'three weeks' in Chinese?
What measure word goes between the number and 星期?
